wine_museums.name: Poli Museo della Grappa - Bassano del Grappa
wine_museums.address: Via Gamba 6, 36061 Bassano del Grappa (VI), Italy
wine_museums.admission: Free; EUR3.50 for guided tour with 5-spirit tasting
wine_museums.opening_hours: Daily 9:00-19:30; closed 1 January, Easter Sunday, 25 December
wine_museums.collection_focus: Evolution of distillation; 1,500+ miniature grappa bottle collection from 323 distilleries; Veneto grappa heritage
wine_museums.description: Housed in the historic Palazzo delle Teste opposite the Ponte Vecchio, the Poli Grappa Museum traces the art of grappa distillation through five interactive rooms. The miniature bottle collection of approximately 1,500 pieces from 323 producers is the most complete in Italy. Free entry; guided tasting tour EUR3.50.
wine_museums.tip: The guided tour (EUR3.50) includes five grappa tastings - better value than buying individual glasses at the Grappateca.

verified.address_quoted: Via Gamba 6 (Ponte Vecchio) Bassano del Grappa (VI) - Italia

wine_museums.name: Poli Grappateca Storica - Schiavon
wine_museums.admission: Included in distillery tours; EUR10-35 depending on tour tier
wine_museums.collection_focus: 2,000 historic grappa bottles from 1930s-1980s by 440 producers; active working distillery adjacent
wine_museums.description: The second Poli museum at the Schiavon distillery holds the world's most significant historic grappa archive: approximately 2,000 regular-size bottles from 440 different producers, spanning the 1930s to 1980s. Accessible only through distillery tour bookings. The archive chronicles the evolution of Italy's grappa industry.
wine_museums.tip: The 1940s-era bottles show how wartime alcohol restrictions shaped regional grappa styles - a unique material record.

wine_museums.name: Museo del Vino - Villa Canestrari
wine_museums.admission: By advance reservation; contact marketing@villacanestrari.com or +39 045 7650074
wine_museums.opening_hours: By reservation; visits open to all groups
wine_museums.collection_focus: Four generations of Canestrari winemaking; mechanical 20th-century equipment; Amarone Riserva ageing cellar; wine-analysis instruments
wine_museums.description: Set up to celebrate four generations of the Canestrari winemaking family in the Val d'Illasi. Displays mechanical equipment from the early 20th century, wine-analysis instruments, and a stone cellar where Amarone Riserva ages. Reservation-only visits for individuals and groups.
wine_museums.tip: Combine with a tasting of the current release Canestrari Amarone Riserva - the museum cellar holds the same wine ageing behind the glass.

wine_museums.name: Distilleria Nardini - Historic Production Rooms
wine_museums.address: Via Madonna di Monte Berico 7, 36061 Bassano del Grappa (VI), Italy
wine_museums.admission: Included in guided tour; reservation via visite@nardini.it
wine_museums.collection_focus: 18th-century copper pot stills in active use; production archive of Italy's oldest grappa distillery since 1779
wine_museums.description: The main Nardini production facility preserves 18th-century copper pot stills in continuous operation since 1779. Guided tours reveal the intact historical rooms and end with a tasting flight. Not a museum in the formal sense but unique in showing living 18th-century industrial heritage.
wine_museums.tip: The Grapperia sul Ponte (Ponte Vecchio 2) is the tasting bar; the main site is the production archive. Visit both for the complete picture.

verified.address_quoted: Via Madonna di Monte Berico 7 - 36061 Bassano del Grappa (VI)

wine_museums.name: Cantine Bertani - Historic Amarone Cellar
wine_museums.admission: Valpolicella Classica Experience EUR70; Library Experience EUR200; book via bertani.net
wine_museums.opening_hours: By appointment; open for Cantine Aperte (late May) and other events
wine_museums.collection_focus: 45 vintages of Amarone della Valpolicella Classico from 1958-2015; historic tasting rooms in restored winery
wine_museums.description: Bertani's historic Grezzana cellars hold 45 vintages of Amarone spanning 1958-2015, making it the most significant Amarone vertical archive accessible to the public in Veneto. The Library Experience (EUR200) provides guided access to select back-vintages in restored tasting rooms. Centennial charm with contemporary design.
wine_museums.tip: This is not a dedicated museum but the most significant wine-archive visitor experience in the Veneto. Cantine Aperte in late May unlocks rarely opened vintages.
